data.title: Google Seeks Dismissal of Antitrust Lawsuit Over AI Search Summaries
data.description: According to PANews, Google has filed a motion with the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia to dismiss an antitrust lawsuit brought by Penske Media regarding its AI search summaries. Google argues that AI Overviews are a legitimate product enhancement and that media outlets have the option to opt out of being indexed, thus not constituting a coercive action. This marks Google's third attempt to have the case dismissed. If the motion is denied, the lawsuit may proceed to a broader antitrust examination concerning AI and platform power.

data.title: Crypto Trader Huang Licheng Closes ZEC Position and Opens New Leverage Trades
data.description: According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, on January 14, crypto trader Huang Licheng, also known as 'Brother Ma Ji,' closed a ZEC long position, securing a profit of $6,256. Following this, he opened new leveraged positions, including a 25x leverage long position on 9,000 ETH, with a current unrealized profit of $1.376 million, and a 10x leverage long position on 269,000 HYPE, with an unrealized profit of $107,000.

data.title: Metaplanet's Stock Surges Over 14% in Japan
data.description: According to Odaily, Metaplanet, a publicly listed Bitcoin treasury company in Japan, saw its stock price increase by more than 14% today, currently trading at 605 yen.

data.title: Bitcoin OG Whales Show Decreased Activity Amid Market Changes
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, on January 14, CryptoQuant analyst Darkfost noted a significant decline in the on-chain activity of Bitcoin OG whales, who are holders that have not moved their coins for over five years. During this cycle, the activity of these OG whales was unusually high, with the number of UTXOs spent surpassing levels from the previous cycle.

It is also noteworthy that this cycle provided Bitcoin OG whales with an almost perfect selling window, as large institutional funds and even government-level buyers entered the market. As the cycle progressed, the selling behavior of Bitcoin OG whales at periodic highs has been steadily decreasing.

The 90-day moving average of the most recent STXO peak was approximately 2,300 BTC, but this average has since dropped significantly, currently fluctuating around 1,000 BTC. This indicates that the selling pace of Bitcoin OG whales has also slowed down. The impactful selling pressure is noticeably decreasing, with the current dominant trend leaning more towards holding rather than distributing (selling).

data.title: Vitalik Buterin: Ethereum has achieved the original Web3 vision
data.description:
Vitalik Buterin believes Ethereum has the full Web3 tech stack that was envisioned back in 2014, with a mix of L1 features and side projects.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/vitalik-buterin-ethereum-web3-vision/

data.title: Ripple Secures Preliminary EMI Approval in Luxembourg, Strengthening EU Expansion
data.description:
Ripple announced that it has received preliminary approval for an Electronic Money Institution (EMI) license from Luxembourg’s financial regulator, the Commission de Surveillance du Secteur Financier (CSSF). The development marks a key regulatory milestone in Ripple’s plan to build institutional-grade digital asset infrastructure across Europe and scale Ripple Payments throughout the European Union.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/ripple-secures-preliminary-emi-approval-in-luxembourg-strengthening-eu-expansion/

data.title: A whale deposited 2,015 ETH into HTX after a three-month hiatus, incurring a loss of $2.04 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale (0x6F3...31Bc) deposited 2015 ETH, worth $6.67 million, into HTX after being dormant for three months. This whale initially withdrew the ETH for $8.73 million, resulting in a loss of $2.04 million from this transaction.

data.title: Over $130 billion flows into crypto market in 24 hours
data.description:
The cryptocurrency market added more than $130 billion in value over the past 24 hours, reflecting a renewed inflow of capital into digital assets. As of press time, total crypto market capitalization stood at $3.24 trillion, up from $3.11 trillion a day earlier, a net increase of roughly $130 billion
source: https://finbold.com/over-130-billion-flows-into-crypto-market-in-24-hours/

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin's gains in 2026 were concentrated during North American trading hours, while Asian trading hours dragged down its overall performance.
data.description: Bitcoin briefly surged to $96,000, marking a nearly 10% increase since 2026. This upward trend was primarily driven by strong performance during North American trading hours. According to Velo data, Bitcoin's cumulative return during North American trading hours was approximately 8%. In contrast, European trading hours saw only a modest increase of about 3%, while Asian trading hours dragged down the overall performance. This trend contrasts sharply with the situation at the end of 2025, when Bitcoin fell by as much as 20% during North American trading hours at the end of November, hitting a low of nearly $80,000. In the fourth quarter, Bitcoin selling pressure frequently emerged at the opening of the US market, and spot Bitcoin ETFs faced almost daily outflows. The strongest returns so far have occurred shortly after the US market opens, a period that coincides with Bitcoin's weakest performance over the past six months. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Bitget, in partnership with World Cup champion Julián Álvarez, released a promotional video outlining the UEX strategic vision.
data.description: Bitget recently released a new series of promotional videos starring World Cup champion Julián Álvarez, vividly interpreting its Unlimited Asset Exchange (UEX) concept through football culture. The videos visualize the complex logic of asset allocation as a stylized football equipment store, transforming diverse assets such as crypto assets, tokenized stocks, gold, and forex into sporting elements like footballs, shin guards, and boots. This intuitively conveys the core strategic intent of "one-stop trading of global quality assets," helping users allocate funds more efficiently across different asset classes. Bitget's Chief Marketing Officer, Ignacio Aguirre, stated, "Just as professional football players need the most suitable equipment in the same professional venue, Bitget provides traders with seamless access to diverse assets within a single platform. Compared to traditional trading methods that require frequent platform switching, UEX emphasizes the integration of asset coverage, execution efficiency, and operational flexibility, and this convenience itself is a competitive advantage."
